comments.body: infunclass is a local class. Local classes cannot be static... well, not in many Java versions until recently afaik
please explain in detail with all the rules These rules are explained many places, most authoritatively in the Java Language Specification.

answers.body:
Can we declare a static class inside a static function in java?

No. The JLS forbids it. The governing parts of the specification are as follows:

"The modifier static pertains only to member classes (§8.5.1), not to top level or local or anonymous classes." JLS 8.1.1

"A member class is a class whose declaration is directly enclosed in the body of another class or interface declaration (§8.1.6, §9.1.4). " JLS 8.5

A class declared directly within a method is a local class, not a member class

comments.body: A static class is a member of another class, not of a method.    Methods are executed at runtime (on the stack).    Static classes belong to the class level (compile-time) — so they can’t exist inside a method body.
Please edit your answer to include your explanation, instead of leaving it as a comment.

answers.body:
public class inner {
    static class in {
        static void inclass() {
           
            class infunclass {
                void fun() {
                    System.out.println("Infunclass");
                }
            }

            infunclass obj = new infunclass();
            obj.fun();
        }

        static void infun() {
            System.out.println("Infun");
            inclass();
        }
    }

    static void fun() {
        System.out.println("fun");
        in.infun();
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        fun();
    }
}

title: Can we declare a static class inside a static function in java?
body:
What is wrong with the bellow code? And if something is wrong, please explain in detail with all the rules of static class and functions in java.

public class inner 
    {
        static class in
        {
            static void inclass()
            {
               static class infunclass
                {
                   static void fun()
                    {
                        System.out.println("Infunclass");
                    }
                }
                infunclass.fun();
            }
            static void infun()
            {
                System.out.println("Infun");
                inclass();
            }
        }    
        static void fun()
        {
            System.out.println("fun");
            in.infun();
        }
        public static void main(String[] args)
        {
            fun();
        }
    }

Now when I remove the static keyword before the inclassfun, it showing a different error

public class inner 
{
    static class in
    {
        static void inclass()
        {
           class infunclass
            {
               static void fun()
                {
                    System.out.println("Infunclass");
                }
            }
           infunclass.fun();
        }
        static void infun()
        {
            System.out.println("Infun");
            inclass();
        }
    }    
    static void fun()
    {
        System.out.println("fun");
        in.infun();
    }
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        fun();
    }
}

static void fun() ^ modifier 'static' is only allowed in constant variable declarations
